[[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)}} in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun period]] [[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)}} in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.

A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:円墳]] }} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
[[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft}} in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=draft}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群|12=draft}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:円墳]] }} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]]

Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. [[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft}} in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=draft}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群|12=draft}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:円墳]] }} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=draft}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群|12=draft}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]] {{Kofun navbar}}

However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=draft}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群|12=draft}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
[[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft}} in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=draft}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群|12=draft}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

[[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft}} in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=draft}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群|12=draft}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|横穴式石室|" to "|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|"
revision.text:
[[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=draft|12=draft}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]]

The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]]

revision.comment: 76 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:円墳 up to rev 101020703
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|愛知県名古屋市の[[八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|八幡山古墳]]は野球場のグラウンドほどの面積がある。
{{国土航空写真}}]] '''円墳'''（えんぷん）は、[[古墳]]の[[墳丘墓|墳丘]]形式の一種であり、平面が円形の古墳をいう。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:Gyoda Maruhakayama Tumulus In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|最大の円墳とされていた[[丸墓山古墳]]]] [[古墳時代]]を通じてつくられ、直径数メートルから百メートル前後で、規模は中・小型のものが多い。 墳形が単純なので、時期による形態の変遷は明確でない。墳頂部に墓壙を掘る前・中期のものは墳頂部の平坦面が広いものが多く、後期には土饅頭系が増え、[[横穴式石室]]をもつ後期や終末期のものでは墳頂平坦面が狭く、墳高がやや高い傾向にある。 なお、円墳に造出を付けたものを[[帆立貝形古墳]]と呼ぶ。[[前方後円墳]]の前方部が小さいものと解されることもあるが、造出の規模の小さいものは円墳に分類することが多い『知識ゼロからの古墳入門』70頁 広瀬 和雄 （監修） 『知識ゼロからの古墳入門』71頁 広瀬 和雄 （監修） 。 最大の円墳は[[埼玉古墳群]]中にある[[丸墓山古墳]]（墳径105m）とされてきた。しかし[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある円墳の[[富雄丸山古墳]]を市[[教育委員会]]が[[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）に測量調査した結果、従来直径86ｍとされていたこの古墳が直径110ｍ前後に復元できることが判明した[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html 古墳・埴輪窯] - 奈良市（2018年5月18日 午前5時50分（[[日本標準時|JST]]）閲覧）。そうなれば富雄丸山古墳が丸墓山古墳を抜いて最大の円墳となる。ちなみに[[前方後円墳]]最大の[[大山古墳]]の後円部径は250メートルであり、後円部径が100メートルを超える前方後円墳は約50基を数える田中琢・佐原真編『日本考古学事典』白石太一郎の円墳の項を参照した。。 == 各地の円墳 == 各地の代表的な円墳の名前・読み仮名・所在地・時期・規模・その他など === 東北・北海道地方 === #[[江釣子古墳群]]（えつりこ、岩手県[[北上市]]上江釣子・北鬼柳・和賀町長沼、4支群があり、直径10メートル前後、高さ1メートルほどの円墳が約120基ほど確認されている。古墳時代後期後半の群集墳[[#高橋|高橋(1991) 28ページ]]。[[末期古墳]]、国の史跡） #[[高瀬山古墳]]（たかせやまこふん、[[山形県]][[寒河江市]]、7世紀前後、周溝外周径34メートル、周溝内周径23メートル、高さ1.3メートル、山形県史跡） #小塚古墳（宮城県名取市、直径54メートル、[[雷神山古墳]]の後円部の北に接する。国の史跡） #[[甲塚古墳 (いわき市)|甲塚古墳]]（かぶとづかこふん、福島県いわき市、直径37メートル、高さ約8.2メートル、国の史跡） === 関東地方 === # [[車塚古墳 (壬生町)|車塚古墳]]（くるまづかこふん、栃木県下都賀郡壬生町、終末期、直径約44.7メートル直径82メートル（広瀬和雄『前方後円墳の世界』〈岩波新書1264〉2010年8月 166ページ）、国の史跡） # 埼玉[[丸墓山古墳]]（さきたままるはかやまこふん、埼玉県[[行田市]]、埼玉古墳群、墳径105メートル、墳高18.9メートル「ガイドブックさきたま」3頁 埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館発行 2010年11月、日本最大とされてきたが第2位である可能性、国の史跡） # [[甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|甲山古墳]]（かぶとやまこふん、埼玉県[[熊谷市]]、墳径90メートル、墳高11.25メートル、埼玉県史跡） # [[芝山古墳群]]（しばやまこふんぐん、千葉県山武郡横芝光町、群中に13基の円墳、国の史跡） # [[車塚古墳 (大洗町)|車塚古墳]]（くるまづかこふん、茨城県東茨城郡大洗町、直径約95メートル、茨城県史跡）直径約95メートル（大洗町史編さん委員会『大洗町史（通史編）』1986年3月 182ページ） === 北陸地方 === #[[城の山古墳]]（じょうのやまこふん、新潟県胎内市塩津、直径40メートル） #[[水科古墳群]]（みずしなこふんぐん、新潟県上越市水科、群集墳、国の史跡） #[[兜山古墳 (鯖江市)|兜山古墳]]（かぶとやまこふん、福井県鯖江市、直径60メートル、国の史跡、福井県最大の円墳） #[[春日山古墳 (永平寺町)|春日山古墳]]（かすがやまこふん、福井県吉田郡永平寺町、直径約20メートル） === 中部・東海地方 === #[[兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|兜塚古墳]]（かぶとづかこふん、静岡県磐田市見付、古墳時代中期、直径80メートル、県内最大） #[[八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|八幡山古墳]]（はちまんやまこふん、愛知県名古屋市昭和区山脇町、古墳時代中期（5世紀半ば）、直径約74メートル（現状約85メートル）、東海地方最大、1931年（昭和6年）国の史跡に指定） === 近畿地方 === #[[富雄丸山古墳]]（とみおまるやまこふん、奈良県奈良市丸山1丁目、[[4世紀]]後半、直径86ｍとされてきたが直径110ｍ前後に復元すれば日本最大） #[[安倍文殊院#境内|文殊院西古墳]]（もんじゅいんにしこふん、奈良県桜井市阿部金蔵645番中、古墳時代終末期の大型円墳、国の特別史跡） #[[牧野古墳]]（ばくやこふん、奈良県北葛城郡広陵町馬見北八丁目四番、径55メートルの大円墳、被葬者に[[押坂彦人大兄]]を当てる説がある。国の史跡） === 中国地方 === #[[スクモ塚古墳]]（すくもづかこふん、島根県益田市久城町字須久茂塚、大型の円墳として国の史跡に指定されたが、[[前方後円墳]]とも考えられている） #[[上塩冶築山古墳]]（かみえんやつきやまこふん、島根県[[出雲市]]、今市・塩冶古墳群、6世紀末、国の史跡） #[[上塩冶地蔵山古墳]]（かみえんやじぞうやまこふん、島根県出雲市、今市・塩冶古墳群、6世紀末、国の史跡） #[[明神古墳]]（みょうじんこふん、島根県大田市、6世紀後半、10.8メートルの横穴式石室、副葬品に金メッキの飾りを付けた刀、島根県史跡） #[[三明寺古墳]]（さんみょうじこふん、鳥取県倉吉市、後期、直径18メートル、山陰地方最大級の[[横穴式石室]]をもつ、国の史跡） #[[岡田山古墳|岡田山2号墳]]（おかだやまにごうふん、島根県松江市大草町岡田、6世紀後半、2段築成で径44メートル。国の史跡） === 四国地方 === #[[段の塚穴]] - （だんのつかあな）、徳島県[[美馬市]]、古墳時代後期の次の2基の古墳から成る。国の史跡 #*太鼓塚 - 東西37メートル、南北33メートル、高さ10メートル #*棚塚 - 径20メートル、高さ7メートル === 九州・沖縄地方 === #[[千代丸古墳]]（ちよまるこふん） #[[御塚・権現塚古墳#権現塚古墳|権現塚古墳]]（ごんげんづかこふん） - 福岡県久留米市、直径約50メートル、高さ9メートル、周濠が二重に巡る。円筒、人物・家形などの形象埴輪が採集されている。築造時期不明。国の史跡　 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[永原慶二]]監修 [[石上英一]]他編集『岩波　日本史辞典』[[岩波書店]] 1999年 ISBN 4-00-080093-0 * [[田中琢]]・[[佐原真]]編『日本考古学事典』[[三省堂]]　2003年　ISBN 4-385-15835-5 * {{Cite book | 和書|author=高橋信雄|authorlink=高橋信雄 (考古学者)|year=1991 |chapter=江釣子古墳 |editor=文化庁文化財保護部史跡研究会監修|editor-link=文化庁 |title=図説　日本の史跡　第2巻　原始2 |publisher=[[同朋舎|同朋舎出版]] |isbn=978-4-8104-0925-3 |ref=高橋}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[日本の史跡一覧]] {{Japanese-history-stub|えんふん}} {{DEFAULTSORT:えんふん}} [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:古墳]] [[Category:円墳|*]]

最大の円墳は[[埼玉古墳群]]中にある[[丸墓山古墳]]（墳径105m）とされてきた。しかし[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある円墳の[[富雄丸山古墳]]を市[[教育委員会]]が[[2017年]]（[[平成]]29年）に測量調査した結果、従来直径86ｍとされていたこの古墳が直径110ｍ前後に復元できることが判明した[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html 古墳・埴輪窯] - 奈良市（2018年5月18日 午前5時50分（[[日本標準時|JST]]）閲覧）。そうなれば富雄丸山古墳が丸墓山古墳を抜いて最大の円墳となる。ちなみに[[前方後円墳]]最大の[[大山古墳]]の後円部径は250メートルであり、後円部径が100メートルを超える前方後円墳は約50基を数える田中琢・佐原真編『日本考古学事典』白石太一郎の円墳の項を参照した。。 == 各地の円墳 == 各地の代表的な円墳の名前・読み仮名・所在地・時期・規模・その他など === 東北・北海道地方 === #[[江釣子古墳群]]（えつりこ、岩手県[[北上市]]上江釣子・北鬼柳・和賀町長沼、4支群があり、直径10メートル前後、高さ1メートルほどの円墳が約120基ほど確認されている。古墳時代後期後半の群集墳[[#高橋|高橋(1991) 28ページ]]。[[末期古墳]]、国の史跡） #[[高瀬山古墳]]（たかせやまこふん、[[山形県]][[寒河江市]]、7世紀前後、周溝外周径34メートル、周溝内周径23メートル、高さ1.3メートル、山形県史跡） #小塚古墳（宮城県名取市、直径54メートル、[[雷神山古墳]]の後円部の北に接する。国の史跡） #[[甲塚古墳 (いわき市)|甲塚古墳]]（かぶとづかこふん、福島県いわき市、直径37メートル、高さ約8.2メートル、国の史跡） === 関東地方 === # [[車塚古墳 (壬生町)|車塚古墳]]（くるまづかこふん、栃木県下都賀郡壬生町、終末期、直径約44.7メートル直径82メートル（広瀬和雄『前方後円墳の世界』〈岩波新書1264〉2010年8月 166ページ）、国の史跡） # 埼玉[[丸墓山古墳]]（さきたままるはかやまこふん、埼玉県[[行田市]]、埼玉古墳群、墳径105メートル、墳高18.9メートル「ガイドブックさきたま」3頁 埼玉県立さきたま史跡の博物館発行 2010年11月、日本最大とされてきたが第2位である可能性、国の史跡） # [[甲山古墳 (熊谷市)|甲山古墳]]（かぶとやまこふん、埼玉県[[熊谷市]]、墳径90メートル、墳高11.25メートル、埼玉県史跡） # [[芝山古墳群]]（しばやまこふんぐん、千葉県山武郡横芝光町、群中に13基の円墳、国の史跡） # [[車塚古墳 (大洗町)|車塚古墳]]（くるまづかこふん、茨城県東茨城郡大洗町、直径約95メートル、茨城県史跡）直径約95メートル（大洗町史編さん委員会『大洗町史（通史編）』1986年3月 182ページ） === 北陸地方 === #[[城の山古墳]]（じょうのやまこふん、新潟県胎内市塩津、直径40メートル） #[[水科古墳群]]（みずしなこふんぐん、新潟県上越市水科、群集墳、国の史跡） #[[兜山古墳 (鯖江市)|兜山古墳]]（かぶとやまこふん、福井県鯖江市、直径60メートル、国の史跡、福井県最大の円墳） #[[春日山古墳 (永平寺町)|春日山古墳]]（かすがやまこふん、福井県吉田郡永平寺町、直径約20メートル） === 中部・東海地方 === #[[兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|兜塚古墳]]（かぶとづかこふん、静岡県磐田市見付、古墳時代中期、直径80メートル、県内最大） #[[八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|八幡山古墳]]（はちまんやまこふん、愛知県名古屋市昭和区山脇町、古墳時代中期（5世紀半ば）、直径約74メートル（現状約85メートル）、東海地方最大、1931年（昭和6年）国の史跡に指定） === 近畿地方 === #[[富雄丸山古墳]]（とみおまるやまこふん、奈良県奈良市丸山1丁目、[[4世紀]]後半、直径86ｍとされてきたが直径110ｍ前後に復元すれば日本最大） #[[安倍文殊院#境内|文殊院西古墳]]（もんじゅいんにしこふん、奈良県桜井市阿部金蔵645番中、古墳時代終末期の大型円墳、国の特別史跡） #[[牧野古墳]]（ばくやこふん、奈良県北葛城郡広陵町馬見北八丁目四番、径55メートルの大円墳、被葬者に[[押坂彦人大兄]]を当てる説がある。国の史跡） === 中国地方 === #[[スクモ塚古墳]]（すくもづかこふん、島根県益田市久城町字須久茂塚、大型の円墳として国の史跡に指定されたが、[[前方後円墳]]とも考えられている） #[[上塩冶築山古墳]]（かみえんやつきやまこふん、島根県[[出雲市]]、今市・塩冶古墳群、6世紀末、国の史跡） #[[上塩冶地蔵山古墳]]（かみえんやじぞうやまこふん、島根県出雲市、今市・塩冶古墳群、6世紀末、国の史跡） #[[明神古墳]]（みょうじんこふん、島根県大田市、6世紀後半、10.8メートルの横穴式石室、副葬品に金メッキの飾りを付けた刀、島根県史跡） #[[三明寺古墳]]（さんみょうじこふん、鳥取県倉吉市、後期、直径18メートル、山陰地方最大級の[[横穴式石室]]をもつ、国の史跡） #[[岡田山古墳|岡田山2号墳]]（おかだやまにごうふん、島根県松江市大草町岡田、6世紀後半、2段築成で径44メートル。国の史跡） === 四国地方 === #[[段の塚穴]] - （だんのつかあな）、徳島県[[美馬市]]、古墳時代後期の次の2基の古墳から成る。国の史跡 #*太鼓塚 - 東西37メートル、南北33メートル、高さ10メートル #*棚塚 - 径20メートル、高さ7メートル === 九州・沖縄地方 === #[[千代丸古墳]]（ちよまるこふん） #[[御塚・権現塚古墳#権現塚古墳|権現塚古墳]]（ごんげんづかこふん） - 福岡県久留米市、直径約50メートル、高さ9メートル、周濠が二重に巡る。円筒、人物・家形などの形象埴輪が採集されている。築造時期不明。国の史跡　 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[永原慶二]]監修 [[石上英一]]他編集『岩波　日本史辞典』[[岩波書店]] 1999年 ISBN 4-00-080093-0 * [[田中琢]]・[[佐原真]]編『日本考古学事典』[[三省堂]]　2003年　ISBN 4-385-15835-5 * {{Cite book | 和書|author=高橋信雄|authorlink=高橋信雄 (考古学者)|year=1991 |chapter=江釣子古墳 |editor=文化庁文化財保護部史跡研究会監修|editor-link=文化庁 |title=図説　日本の史跡　第2巻　原始2 |publisher=[[同朋舎|同朋舎出版]] |isbn=978-4-8104-0925-3 |ref=高橋}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[日本の史跡一覧]] {{Japanese-history-stub|えんふん}} [[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Round Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]] {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[sn:Round Kofun]]}} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]]

In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|qq=}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruhakayama Kofun|qq=}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|qq=}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|qq=}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongen-zuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Japan|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Round Kofun]]}} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|"
revision.text:
[[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|qq=}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruhakayama Kofun|qq=}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruhakayama Kofun|qq=}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisen Kofun|ja|大山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|qq=}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|ja|江釣子古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaseyama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|ja|車塚古墳 (壬生町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|qq=}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|ja|丸墓山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruhakayama Kofun|qq=}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|埼玉古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitama Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|qq=}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|ja|八幡山古墳 (名古屋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|qq=}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|qq=}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Gongen-zuka Kofun|ja|御塚・権現塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gongen-zuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Japan|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Enpun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[sn:Round Kofun]]}} [[sn:Round Kofun]] [[ja:円墳]]
