ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Japan|1=lists of National Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505811}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] }} [[ja:円墳]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[en:Round Kofun]] [[zh:圓墳]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11394747}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[en:Round Kofun]][[ja:円墳]][[zh:圓墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11394747]] Wikidata name: circular kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|[[:en:Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)]] in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|[[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]], formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] (diameter 105m) in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]]. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # [[:en:Ezuriko Kofun Cluster|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster]] (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaseyama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # [[:en:Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)]] (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]]. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134952791}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # [[:en:Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)]] (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # [[:en:Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun|Gongen-zuka Kofun]] (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Japan|1=lists of National Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505811}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] }} [[ja:円墳]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[en:Round Kofun]] [[zh:圓墳]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11394747}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
[[en:Round Kofun]][[ja:円墳]][[zh:圓墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11394747]] Wikidata name: circular kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|[[:en:Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)]] in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|[[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]], formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] (diameter 105m) in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]]. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # [[:en:Ezuriko Kofun Cluster|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster]] (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaseyama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # [[:en:Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)]] (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]]. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134952791}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # [[:en:Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)]] (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # [[:en:Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun|Gongen-zuka Kofun]] (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Japan|1=lists of National Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505811}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] }} [[ja:円墳]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[en:Round Kofun]] [[zh:圓墳]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{wikidata link|Q11394747}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|[[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]], formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] (diameter 105m) in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]]. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # [[:en:Ezuriko Kofun Cluster|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster]] (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaseyama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # [[:en:Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)]] (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]]. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134952791}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # [[:en:Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)]] (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # [[:en:Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun|Gongen-zuka Kofun]] (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Japan|1=lists of National Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505811}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:円墳]] [[en:Round Kofun]] [[zh:圓墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11394747}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

While sometimes regarded as miniature [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] (diameter 105m) in the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]]. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # [[:en:Ezuriko Kofun Cluster|Etsuriko Kofun Cluster]] (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaseyama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # [[:en:Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)]] (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # [[:en:Maruhakayama Kofun|Maruhakayama Kofun]] (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the [[:en:Sakitama Kofun Cluster|Sakitama Kofun Cluster]]. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134952791}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # [[:en:Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)]] (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # [[:en:Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun|Gongen-zuka Kofun]] (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Japan|1=lists of National Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505811}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:円墳]] [[en:Round Kofun]] [[zh:圓墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11394747}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[en:Round Kofun]][[ja:円墳]][[zh:圓墳]] [[en:Round Kofun]][[ja:円墳]][[zh:圓墳]] [[en:Round Kofun]][[ja:円墳]][[zh:圓墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11394747]] Wikidata name: circular kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|en|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|WD=Q17218723|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)}} in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|en|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Ezuriko Kofun Cluster|en|Ezuriko Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11551374|lt=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaseyama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|en|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|WD=Q17990657|lt=Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|en|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134952791}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|en|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|WD=Q17218723|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun|en|Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun|WD=Q11488904|lt=Gongen-zuka Kofun}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Japan|1=lists of National Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505811}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:円墳]] [[en:Round Kofun]] [[zh:圓墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11394747}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|en|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Ezuriko Kofun Cluster|en|Ezuriko Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11551374|lt=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaseyama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|en|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|WD=Q17990657|lt=Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|en|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134952791}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|en|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|WD=Q17218723|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun|en|Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun|WD=Q11488904|lt=Gongen-zuka Kofun}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Japan|1=lists of National Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505811}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:円墳]] [[en:Round Kofun]] [[zh:圓墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11394747}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|en|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Ezuriko Kofun Cluster|en|Ezuriko Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11551374|lt=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaseyama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Japan|1=lists of National Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505811}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:円墳]] [[en:Round Kofun]] [[zh:圓墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11394747}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[en:Round Kofun]][[ja:円墳]][[zh:圓墳]] [[en:Round Kofun]][[ja:円墳]][[zh:圓墳]] [[en:Round Kofun]][[ja:円墳]][[zh:圓墳]] [[File:Hachimanyama kofun-Aichi02.jpg|thumb|250px|{{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|en|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|WD=Q17218723|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|1=Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)}} in Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture, is as large as a baseball field.
{{ill|National Land Aerial Photograph|ja|国土航空写真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Land Aerial Photograph|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491397|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}}]] '''Enpun''' (円墳) refers to a type of tumulus with a circular plan, characteristic of [[kofun]]. == Overview == [[File:Gyoda Maruhakayama Kofun In Spring from afar.jpg|thumb|230px|right|{{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun|1=Maruhakayama Kofun}}, formerly regarded as the largest enpun]] Constructed throughout the [[Kofun period]], enpun range from a few meters to over 100 meters in diameter, with most examples being medium- to small-sized. Due to its simple shape, changes in enpun morphology over time are not well-defined. Early and mid-period examples often feature a flat summit where a burial pit was excavated. Later examples from the terminal Kofun period tend to have dome-like shapes, narrower flat summits, and slightly higher mounds, with {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|lt=corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. Enpun with added projections are classified as {{ill|Hotategai-gata Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotategai-gata Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. While sometimes regarded as miniature {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, smaller examples are usually categorized as enpun.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 70.'Introduction to Kofun for Beginners,' Hirose Kazuo (ed.), p. 71. The largest enpun has long been considered the {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun|1=Maruhakayama Kofun}} (diameter 105m) in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|en|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}}. However, in 2017, the {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} in Nara City, previously measured at 86m in diameter, was found through surveys to be approximately 110m in diameter, making it the largest enpun.[http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1182152154399/index.html Kofun and Haniwa Kilns] - Nara City (accessed May 18, 2018, 5:50 JST). In comparison, the rear circle of the largest zenpō-kōen-fun, {{ill|Daisenryō Kofun|ja|大山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大仙陵古墳}}, measures 250m in diameter, and approximately 50 zenpō-kōen-fun have rear circles exceeding 100m.Refer to the "Enpun" entry by Taichirō Shiraishi in 'Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology,' edited by Takashi Tanaka and Makoto Sahara. == Regional Examples of Enpun == Below are representative enpun categorized by region, including names, readings, locations, periods, dimensions, and notable features: === Tōhoku and Hokkaidō === # {{ill|Ezuriko Kofun Cluster|en|Ezuriko Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11551374|lt=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|1=Ezuriko Kofun Cluster}} (Iwate Prefecture, Kitakami): Approximately 120 enpun, each about 10m in diameter, dating to the late Kofun period. Designated a National Historic Site.{{Cite book|author=Nobuo Takahashi|chapter=Etsuriko Kofun Cluster|title=Illustrated History of Japan, Volume 2: Early Times}} # {{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|lt=Takaseyama Kofun|ja|高瀬山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaseyama Kofun|qq=}} (Yamagata Prefecture, Sagae City): Diameter 34m, from the 7th century. A Yamagata Prefectural Historic Site. === Kantō === # {{ill|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|en|Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)|WD=Q17990657|lt=Kuruma-zuka Kofun (Mibu Town)|1=Kurumazuka Kofun (Mibu)}} (Tochigi Prefecture, Mibu): Diameter 44.7m, National Historic Site. # {{ill|Maruhakayama Kofun|en|Maruhakayama Kofun|WD=Q11368078|lt=Maruhakayama Kofun|1=Maruhakayama Kofun}} (Saitama Prefecture, Gyōda): Diameter 105m, historically considered the largest enpun. Part of the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|en|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q2213580|lt=Sakitama Kofun Cluster|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}}. === Chūbu and Tōkai === # {{ill|Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja|兜塚古墳 (磐田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kabutozuka Kofun (Iwata)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Q134952791|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134952791}} (Shizuoka Prefecture, Iwata): Diameter 80m, largest in Shizuoka Prefecture. # {{ill|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|en|Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|WD=Q17218723|lt=Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)|1=Hachimanyama Kofun (Nagoya)}} (Aichi Prefecture, Nagoya): Diameter 74m, largest in the Tōkai region. Designated a National Historic Site in 1931. === Near Kyoto/Nara === # {{ill|Tomio Maruyama Kofun|ja|富雄丸山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomio Maruyama Kofun|1=Tomio-Maruyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28688548}} (Nara City): Diameter approximately 110m, potentially the largest enpun in Japan. === Kyūshū === # {{ill|Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun|en|Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun|WD=Q11488904|lt=Gongen-zuka Kofun|1=Ontsuka - Gongenzuka Kofun}} (Fukuoka Prefecture, Kurume City): Diameter 50m, with a double moat and clay figurines. Designated a National Historic Site. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Nagahara Keiji (ed.), Ishigami Eiichi (ed.). ''Iwanami Dictionary of Japanese History.'' Iwanami Shoten, 1999. ISBN 4-00-080093-0. * Takashi Tanaka, Makoto Sahara (eds.). ''Dictionary of Japanese Archaeology.'' Sanseidō, 2003. ISBN 4-385-15835-5. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Japan|ja|日本の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Japan|1=lists of National Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505811}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|円墳|version=101020703|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:円墳]] [[en:Round Kofun]] [[zh:圓墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11394747}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Kofun period]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]
